4472	  \label{lem:appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res}

4474	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an open immersion of schemes. On global sections \(f\) induces the comparison
4475	  ring map \(f^{\sharp}_{\top} : \Gamma(Y, \mathcal{O}_Y) \to \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\), and the
4476	  open-immersion section isomorphism
4477	  \(\beta_f : \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X) \xrightarrow{\sim} \Gamma\bigl(f(\top), \mathcal{O}_Y\bigr)\)
4478	  identifies the global sections of \(X\) with the sections of \(Y\) over the image open
4479	  \(f(\top) \subseteq Y\). Then the composite of \(f^{\sharp}_{\top}\) with \(\beta_f^{-1}\) is the
4480	  structure-sheaf restriction of \(Y\) from \(\top\) down to the image open:
4481	  \[
4482	    \beta_f^{-1} \circ f^{\sharp}_{\top} \;=\; \rho^{f(\top)}_{Y}.
4483	  \]
4484	  Equivalently, under the section identification \(\beta_f\) the global-sections comparison map of \(f\)
4485	  is nothing but restriction along \(f(\top) \hookrightarrow Y\).
4486	\end{lemma}

4489	  open. Rewriting the identity in the form \(f^{\sharp}_{\top} = \rho^{f(\top)}_Y \circ \beta_f\) and
